original_hypothesis.title: calculateWithdrawShares Callable During Fulfilled-But-Not-Executed State, Orphaning Calculated Amounts
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The withdraw group state machine allows calculateWithdrawShares to execute when currentWithdrawGroup.isStarted==true AND isFullfilled==true (the fulfilled-but-not-fully-executed state). The guard only blocks when isFullfilled==false AND isStarted==true. New calculations during this window add to totalCBAmount, but when executeWithdraw completes and deletes the withdraw group struct, these accumulated amounts are destroyed. The next withdraw group starts with totalCBAmount=0, missing the calculations done during this window.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The guard condition `if (currentWithdrawGroup.isFullfilled == false && currentWithdrawGroup.isStarted == true) revert` explicitly allows the (true,true) state. Calculations in this window accumulate into totalCBAmount which is destroyed by `delete currentWithdrawGroup`.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Guard at line 206-208 only blocks (isStarted=true, isFullfilled=false); allows (true,true); delete at line 267 destroys orphaned totalCBAmount

preconditions: A withdraw group has been fulfilled but not fully executed (some items remain)
New withdrawal requests have been queued with recordTime <= oldestUpdateTime
Manager calls calculateWithdrawShares before completing executeWithdraw

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Guard allows calculateWithdrawShares when isStarted=true, isFullfilled=true
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Accumulated totalCBAmount destroyed by `delete currentWithdrawGroup` at line 267
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No guard for the (isStarted=true, isFullfilled=true) state

analysis_questions: What happens to calculated withdrawal requests whose amounts are lost?
Do the calculated requests get re-calculated in the next group?
Is there a path where users lose funds due to wrong pro-rata calculations?
validation_requirements: Simulate the sequence: fulfill -> calculateWithdrawShares -> executeWithdraw (completing) -> verify next group state
Check if withdrawQueue.middle advances past requests whose amounts are lost

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Manager fulfills a withdraw group. Before executing all withdrawals, manager calculates new withdrawal requests, advancing withdrawQueue.middle and adding to totalCBAmount. Manager completes executeWithdraw, which deletes the entire currentWithdrawGroup including the newly accumulated totalCBAmount. The next startCurrentWithdrawGroup has totalCBAmount=0 but lastId includes the recently-calculated requests, causing incorrect pro-rata distribution.
prerequisites: Active fulfilled withdraw group with pending execution
New withdrawal requests available for calculation

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Incorrect pro-rata distribution in subsequent withdraw groups; users may receive incorrect amounts
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Users with withdrawals calculated during the vulnerable window
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires manager awareness and careful sequencing

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for calculateWithdrawShares during fulfilled-but-not-executed state

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Requires specific ordering of manager operations
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by monitoring totalCBAmount vs withdrawal queue state

validation_result.mechanism: Insufficient state machine guard allows orphaned withdraw calculations
validation_result.impact_summary: calculateWithdrawShares can execute during fulfilled-but-not-executed state, accumulating totalCBAmount that is destroyed by `delete currentWithdrawGroup`, corrupting subsequent withdrawal group pro-rata distributions and potentially causing fund loss or reverts.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis correctly identifies a defect in the withdraw group state machine. The guard in calculateWithdrawShares (line ~206-208) only blocks when `isFullfilled == false && isStarted == true`, explicitly allowing execution when both are true (fulfilled but not fully executed). During this window:

1. fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup zeroes totalCBAmount and stores the original in totalCBAmountFullfilled
2. calculateWithdrawShares adds new amounts to the zeroed totalCBAmount and advances withdrawQueue.middle
3. executeWithdraw completes and calls `delete currentWithdrawGroup`, destroying the newly accumulated totalCBAmount
4. The next withdraw group starts with totalCBAmount=0, but withdrawQueue.middle has already advanced past the orphaned requests
5. These orphaned requests are included in the next group's execution range (first to lastId) but their amounts are NOT included in totalCBAmountFullfilled, causing incorrect pro-rata calculations or insufficient token balance for transfers.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof of signal accurately identifies the vulnerable code. The guard condition at approximately line 206-208 uses `isFullfilled == false && isStarted == true` which only catches one of two problematic states. The `delete currentWithdrawGroup` at the end of executeWithdraw destroys all struct fields including the newly accumulated totalCBAmount. The totalCBAmount accumulation via `currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmount += assetsNeededForWithdraw` at the end of calculateWithdrawShares directly writes to storage that will be destroyed.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Guard only blocks (isStarted=true, isFullfilled=false) but allows (isStarted=true, isFullfilled=true). This permits calculateWithdrawShares to run after fulfillment but before full execution, accumulating amounts into totalCBAmount that will be destroyed.

Zeroing totalCBAmount after recording totalCBAmountFullfilled creates the window where new calculations write to totalCBAmount starting from 0, making them appear as if they belong to the current group.
The `delete` destroys the entire struct including the totalCBAmount that was accumulated by calculateWithdrawShares during the vulnerable window. The orphaned amounts are permanently lost.
This writes to totalCBAmount (which was zeroed by fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup) and advances withdrawQueue.middle, making it impossible for the next group cycle to re-calculate these requests.

calling_sequence: 1. Normal flow: calculateWithdrawShares → totalCBAmount = X for requests [0..9], middle = 10
3. retrieveTokensForWithdraw → retrieves X tokens from connectors
5. [VULNERABLE WINDOW] calculateWithdrawShares called again → processes requests [10..12], totalCBAmount=Y, middle=13
6. executeWithdraw processes requests [0..9], first advances to 10, lastId==first → delete currentWithdrawGroup (totalCBAmount Y destroyed)
7. Next cycle: startCurrentWithdrawGroup → lastId=13, totalCBAmount=0 (orphaned requests 10-12 not counted)
10. executeWithdraw processes [10..14] with wrong pro-rata ratio Z instead of Y+Z
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The guard at line ~206-208 uses `isFullfilled == false && isStarted == true` which passes when both are true. After fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup sets isFullfilled=true and totalCBAmount=0, calculateWithdrawShares can be called, accumulating new amounts to totalCBAmount and advancing withdrawQueue.middle. When executeWithdraw completes and deletes the struct, these amounts are destroyed. The next group includes these orphaned requests (they're between first and the new lastId) but totalCBAmount doesn't account for them, leading to incorrect totalCBAmountFullfilled and wrong pro-rata distribution in executeWithdraw's baseTokenAmount calculation: `data.amount * currentWithdrawGroup.totalABAmount / currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmountFullfilled`.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that affect this logic. The vulnerability is inherent in the state machine logic.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario is realistic. The manager (keeper) controls the sequencing of calculateWithdrawShares, startCurrentWithdrawGroup, fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup, and executeWithdraw. If the keeper calls calculateWithdrawShares between fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup and the final executeWithdraw (which completes the group), the orphaned calculation bug triggers. This could happen due to operational error or intentional manipulation by a compromised keeper. Even without malicious intent, if new withdrawal requests become eligible during the execution window and the keeper processes them proactively, the bug manifests.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is significant: (1) Orphaned requests are included in the next group but their amounts are not in totalCBAmountFullfilled, causing the pro-rata ratio to be incorrect. If the ratio exceeds 1 (because totalCBAmountFullfilled is too small relative to individual amounts), users may receive more than their fair share but the contract may revert due to insufficient balance. (2) The retrieveTokensForWithdraw function only retrieves based on totalCBAmount, which doesn't include the orphaned amounts, so the contract likely won't have enough tokens to fulfill all withdrawals. (3) Users whose requests were orphaned may end up in a broken state. The resetMiddle function can be used as a manual recovery mechanism if the manager is aware of the issue, but there's no automatic safeguard.
recommended_mitigations: Add the (isStarted=true, isFullfilled=true) state to the guard: `if (currentWithdrawGroup.isStarted == true) { revert NoyaAccounting_ThereIsAnActiveWithdrawGroup(); }`
Alternatively, block calculateWithdrawShares when isStarted is true regardless of isFullfilled: `if (currentWithdrawGroup.isStarted) revert;`
Consider adding an invariant check that withdrawQueue.middle hasn't advanced beyond currentWithdrawGroup.lastId when isFullfilled is true
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy AccountingManager with test tokens. 2. Create multiple withdrawal requests. 3. Execute full flow: calculateWithdrawShares → startCurrentWithdrawGroup → fulfillCurrentWithdrawGroup. 4. Create new withdrawal requests. 5. Call calculateWithdrawShares during the fulfilled-but-not-executed window. 6. Complete executeWithdraw for the first group. 7. Verify currentWithdrawGroup is deleted and totalCBAmount from step 5 is lost. 8. Start new group and verify totalCBAmount doesn't include orphaned amounts. 9. Attempt to execute and observe incorrect pro-rata distribution or revert.
uncertainties: The exact financial impact depends on the relative sizes of orphaned amounts vs. new amounts in the next group
The manager could potentially use resetMiddle to recover, but this requires awareness of the bug and careful manual intervention
additional_testing_needed: End-to-end test of calculateWithdrawShares during fulfilled-but-not-executed window
Test that resetMiddle can recover from the orphaned state
Test what happens to executeWithdraw when totalCBAmountFullfilled is less than the sum of individual request amounts
Fuzz test the withdraw state machine transitions to find other edge cases
